Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:32 ET
P/C split widens as indices load puts while single-names chase calls
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedging — index puts (1.33) protecting single-name call exposure (0.68).
  • 2. Massive SPY call blocks at 710-712 strikes suggest systematic rebalancing into resistance despite put-heavy index flow.
  • 3. UNH unusual two-way flow precedes GDP data as healthcare positioning shifts ahead of policy-sensitive macro prints.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedged bullish

The most striking development is the widening P/C divergence reaching extreme levels, with index puts outnumbering calls 1.33:1 while single-names tilt heavily toward calls at 0.68:1. This 0.65 spread represents institutional dispersion hedging — funds using index puts as portfolio insurance while expressing single-name conviction through calls. The structure makes sense given implied correlation at just 0.04, creating a stock-specific regime where individual names can outperform indices. Systematic flow dominates the options landscape, with SPY seeing massive call blocks at 710-712 strikes totaling over 900k contracts despite the put-heavy index P/C ratio. These strikes sit directly at resistance levels with positive gamma providing $412M in dealer dampening, suggesting systematic rebalancing rather than directional speculation. The combination of put protection and call accumulation at resistance creates a coiled spring setup into GDP data. UNH presents the clearest cross-signal tension with simultaneous 1269% call flow and 1070% put flow increases, generating two-way volatility demand ahead of Tuesday's GDP print. Healthcare remains policy-sensitive to growth data, explaining the vol-buying behavior. Meanwhile, tech names like NVDA and AMD show backwardation despite bullish technicals, pricing elevated near-term event risk into Thursday's TSLA earnings that could spillover to semiconductor momentum.

Layer 1: P/C split 1.33/0.68Layer 2: SPY GEX +$412MLayer 3: UNH two-way flowLayer 9: GDP in 3d
